metadata.title:
The.
warwick Co.

Fair Pleased Them.

pages.raw_text:
The Nelson, b. c., Daily News has
the following concerning the Poplar
mines exhibit here:


IN. j. Morgan and Phil O'Connor Of
the Lucky Jack group have returned
from visit to the Spokane and are
going back to Poplar today. Phil
OConnor was in charge of the Great
Northern mines exhibit at the fair,
with w. b. Pool and Jacob Dover.


Gle says that the exhibit attracted
a great deal of attention from all classes
of visitors. Laymen were struck by the
marvellous richness of the small mug
gets and gazed with wonder on the
chunks of gold that covered the pieces
of rock exhibited, but the old-time
miners, the wise men from California,
looked at the immense specimens of tel
luride and threw up their hands. "


at was something outside their ex-
perence," said O'Connor with chuck
lie, and you can bet that our exhibit ut
Spokane will add hundreds to the boom
rush that is going to strike Poplar
in the spring


"This wonderful exhibit of free gold
ore which has excited so much COM
ment wherever seen is now booked for
the worlds fair at st. Louis. 1t will
probably be the first shown at Winni-
peg, but the company has already prom
ised the st. Louis commissioners that
it will there during the exhibition"
